query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Permeate', 'Spread throughout (something); pervade', 'Critics argued that the plan might permeate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Permissible', 'Allowable', 'The essay highlights the permissible of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Permutation', 'A combination of items made by reordering', 'Her permutation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pernicious', 'Having a harmful effect, especially in a gradual way', 'What seemed pernicious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perpendicular', '(geometry) meeting a given line or surface at right angles', 'Scholars noted a striking perpendicular between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perpetrator', 'A person who commits an illegal or criminal act', 'Her perpetrator became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perpetual', 'Lasting forever, or for an indefinitely long time', 'Scholars noted a striking perpetual between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perpetuate', 'To make something continue indefinitely', '“The media can sometimes perpetuate harmful stereotypes.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perpetuity', 'Endless or indefinitely long duration or existence', 'The essay highlights the perpetuity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perplex', 'To cause to be puzzled or bewildered, confuse mentally', 'Critics argued that the plan might perplex the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perquisite', 'Any monetary or other incidental benefit beyond salary', 'Scholars noted a striking perquisite between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Persecution', 'To punish in a manner designed to injure or afflict', 'Critics argued that the plan might persecution the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Persevere', 'To persist in anything undertaken', 'Over time, the community learned to persevere its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Persiflage', 'Light, bantering talk or writing', 'The essay highlights the persiflage of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Persist', 'To go on steadfastly and resolutely', 'Over time, the community learned to persist its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Persnickety', 'Fastidious or fussy', 'Scholars noted a striking persnickety between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Persona', 'A person''s perceived or evident personality', 'Scholars noted a striking persona between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Personable', 'Having a pleasing manner, friendly, amiable', 'What seemed personable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Personage', 'A person of distinction or importance', 'Scholars noted a striking personage between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Personal', 'Coming from a particular person, individual, private', 'Scholars noted a striking personal between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Personnel', 'Employees, office staff', 'Her personnel became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perspective', 'Depicting spatial relationships on a flat surface', 'The reviewer described the tone as perspective, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perspicacious', 'Having keen insight or understanding', '“The detective was perspicacious, noticing clues that others missed.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perspicacity', 'Keenness of mental perception and understanding', 'Scholars noted a striking perspicacity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perspicuous', 'Clearly expressed and easy to understand', 'Critics argued that the plan might perspicuous the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perspiration', 'Sweat', 'The essay highlights the perspiration of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perspire', 'To emit sweat or perspiration through the skin''s pores', 'Critics argued that the plan might perspire the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Persuadable', 'Capable of being convinced', 'What seemed persuadable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Persuade', 'To successfully convince (someone) to do something', 'Over time, the community learned to persuade its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pert', 'Attractive; impudent or saucy', 'Her pert became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pertain', 'To have reference or relation', 'Critics argued that the plan might pertain the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pertinacious', 'Holding firmly to a course; determined', 'Over time, the community learned to pertinacious its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pertinacity', 'The state or characteristic of being persistent', 'Her pertinacity became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pertinent', 'Relevant or applicable to a particular matter', 'The passage presents a pertinent portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perturb', 'To disturb, to bother or unsettle', 'Over time, the community learned to perturb its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perturbation', 'Agitation, the state of being perturbed', 'Her perturbation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perusal', 'The act of surveying', 'Her perusal became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Peruse', 'To read carefully and thoroughly', 'The author hoped the new policy would peruse tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pervade', 'To become spread through all parts of', 'The author hoped the new policy would pervade tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pervasive', 'Spreading widely throughout an area or group', '“The smell of fresh bread was pervasive in the bakery.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perverse', 'Willfully determined to go counter to what is expected', 'The author hoped the new policy would perverse tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Perversion', 'Humiliation, debasement', 'Scholars noted a striking perversion between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pervert', 'To lead astray morally', 'Over time, the community learned to pervert its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pervious', 'Admitting of passage or entrance', 'The passage presents a pervious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pessimism', 'A general belief that bad things will happen', 'Scholars noted a striking pessimism between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pessimistic', 'Tending to see the worst aspect of things', 'The passage presents a pessimistic portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pestilence', 'A deadly or virulent epidemic disease', 'Her pestilence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pestilent', 'Highly injurious or destructive to life, deadly', 'The author hoped the new policy would pestilent tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Peter', 'To dwindle, to trail off, to diminish to nothing', 'Over time, the community learned to peter its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Petrify', 'To convert into stone or a stony substance', 'The author hoped the new policy would petrify tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
